title: NATO STO Internship 2026 in France: Paid Opportunities for Students and Graduates
summary: The NATO Science and Technology Organization (STO) has officially opened applications for its 2026 internship opportunities at the Collaboration Support Office (CSO) in Neuilly-sur-Seine, Paris, France. These prestigious paid internships provide young professionals and students with the opportunity to work directly within NATO’s international scientific and administrative environment while contributing to defence innovation, logistics, administration, and human resources initiatives. The internship programme is designed for students and recent graduates seeking hands-on international experience in a highly respected global organization. Successful applicants will collaborate with experts from NATO member nations and gain practical exposure to science, technology, operations, logistics, and administration in defence-related sectors. The application deadline is 29 May 2026 . About the NATO STO Collaboration Support Office The Collaboration Support Office (CSO) is one of the executive bodies of the NATO Science and Technology Organization (STO). The office supports scientific and technical committees, collaborative programmes, and innovation initiatives that help strengthen NATO’s defence and security capabilities. More than 5,000 scientists and engineers from NATO and partner nations participate in approximately 460 science and technology activities coordinated through the STO framework. Interns at the CSO become part of this international network and contribute to projects with real-world impact. The internship location is Neuilly-sur-Seine, a suburb west of Paris, France. Available Internship Departments The NATO STO CSO internships are available across several departments, including: 1. Directorate and Office of the Chief of Staff Strategy and policy support Science and Technology Board activities Administrative coordination 2. Operations and Coordination Office Collaborative programme management Scientific committee coordination Science and technology support activities 3. Information and Knowledge Management Support Branches Human Resources Information Management Publications Finance Communications and Information Systems (CIS) Human Resources Internship Responsibilities The Personnel, Administration and Logistics (PAL) Human Resources internship includes responsibilities such as: Updating onboarding and induction materials Supporting HR documentation and handbook creation Assisting with recruitment and payroll tracking tools Conducting HR data analysis and reporting Supporting wellbeing, cohesion, and corporate social responsibility initiatives Managing electronic and paper archiving systems This internship is ideal for applicants interested in Human Resources, Administration, Organizational Development, and International Relations. Logistics and Facility Management Internship Responsibilities The Logistics and Facility Management internship focuses on operational and infrastructure support within the organization. Responsibilities include: Managing inventory and stock databases Supporting logistics and delivery operations Coordinating internal meeting resources Monitoring infrastructure and maintenance activities Supporting service provider coordination Assisting with modernization and process improvement initiatives This role is suitable for candidates with backgrounds in logistics, facilities management, infrastructure, or operational support. Internship Benefits Selected interns will receive several benefits, including: Monthly salary of approximately €1,503 Up to 30 days annual leave (prorated) Work accident insurance coverage Opportunity for contract extension up to one year Experience in a multinational working environment Exposure to NATO’s scientific and defence innovation ecosystem Professional networking with international experts and professionals Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the NATO STO Internship 2026, applicants must: Be nationals of NATO member countries Be at least 21 years old Have completed at least two years of university education or equivalent training Be current students or recent graduates within the last 12 months Possess proficiency in one NATO official language and working knowledge of the other Be eligible for NATO Secret security clearance Additional qualifications such as prior international experience, SharePoint knowledge, HR qualifications, or logistics expertise may strengthen an application. Important Recruitment Notice NATO has emphasized that applications generated fully or partially using generative AI tools, plagiarism, or uncredited sources may be rejected during screening. Applicants are advised to prepare original application documents, including resumes and motivation letters. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the NATO STO Internship 2026 is 29 May 2026 . Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed virtually in mid-June 2026 through phone or Webex. How to Apply Applicants must apply online through the NATO Talent Acquisition Platform (NTAP – Taleo). Candidates should prepare: Resume/CV Motivation letter Educational certificates Applications should be submitted under: Job Number 210251 Collaboration Support Office (CSO) APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more internship opportunities, click here . The post NATO STO Internship 2026 in France: Paid Opportunities for Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: HuMENA Programs Lead Recruitment 2026: Remote Human Rights Leadership Opportunity for MENA Professionals
summary: HuMENA for Human Rights and Civic Engagement is inviting applications for the position of Programs Lead, a senior remote coordination role designed for experienced professionals passionate about civic space, human rights advocacy, and operational leadership across the MENA region. The organization is seeking a highly organized and politically aware professional who can strengthen internal systems, coordinate complex projects, and support sustainable organizational growth. The selected candidate will work closely with HuMENA’s Executive Director and collaborate with teams across Brussels, Beirut, and the wider MENA region. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and the deadline to apply is 21 May 2026 . About HuMENA HuMENA for Human Rights and Civic Engagement is a non-profit and non-partisan organization rooted in the Middle East and North Africa and connected to its diasporas worldwide. Operating from Brussels, Beirut, and across the region, HuMENA focuses on defending civic space, protecting human rights defenders, and supporting marginalized communities facing repression and shrinking freedoms. The organization works extensively with activists, journalists, grassroots organizers, and civil society actors through: Human rights advocacy Capacity-building initiatives Strategic communications Evidence-based research Protection-oriented support International engagement HuMENA’s work prioritizes freedom of expression, peaceful assembly, digital rights, public participation, and protection against reprisals. Position Overview Position Details Role: Programs Lead Location: Remote / MENA Contract Type: Twelve-month consultancy agreement Duration: 12 months, renewable based on funding and performance Time Commitment: Full-time (40 hours weekly) Reporting Line: Executive Director Expected Start Date: 15 June 2026 or earlier Application Deadline: 21 May 2026 Compensation will be determined based on experience, location, and seniority level. Key Responsibilities The Programs Lead will oversee operational coordination and implementation tracking across HuMENA’s projects and partnerships. The role focuses on ensuring that projects, consultants, reporting obligations, and workflows are effectively managed. Main Duties Include: Coordinating project implementation and tracking deliverables Managing internal workflows and operational systems Supporting consultant recruitment and onboarding Monitoring donor reporting deadlines Coordinating advocacy and communications outputs Supporting cross-team collaboration Maintaining project trackers and organizational documentation Identifying operational risks and proposing solutions The successful candidate will help transform scattered processes into structured systems that improve accountability and efficiency across the organization. First 60 Days Expectations Within the first two months, the selected Programs Lead will: Map all active projects, donor obligations, and consultant assignments Build operational project and consultant tracking systems Establish regular internal coordination routines Improve implementation monitoring processes Strengthen organizational workflow visibility Required Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: Minimum 6 years of relevant professional experience At least 3 years in independent coordination or management roles Experience working with MENA-focused civil society or human rights organizations Strong project management and operational coordination skills Fluency in Arabic and professional-level English Experience managing consultants and donor-related deliverables Ability to handle sensitive information professionally Strong remote collaboration skills Preferred Qualifications Additional assets include: Experience in small or growing organizations Familiarity with donor reporting systems Experience with civic space or digital rights advocacy Knowledge of tools like Airtable, Notion, Asana, Trello, or Slack French language proficiency Inclusive Recruitment Commitment HuMENA strongly encourages applications from underrepresented and marginalized communities. The organization welcomes candidates regardless of gender identity, nationality, race, religion, disability, migration status, or socio-economic background. The organization emphasizes contextual understanding, lived experience, and commitment to human rights values alongside formal qualifications. Application Requirements Interested applicants must submit: A CV (maximum three pages) A one-page cover letter Contact information for two professional references One writing sample in Arabic or English Applications should be sent via email with the subject line: Programs Lead — [Candidate Name] Applications and inquiries should be directed to: Jobs@humena.org APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . Recruitment Process Shortlisted candidates will participate in: A first interview A practical assignment lasting approximately 90 minutes A final interview Reference checks Candidates are encouraged to apply early because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Why This Opportunity Matters This role presents a unique opportunity for experienced professionals committed to human rights, civic engagement, and operational leadership in the MENA region. The position allows candidates to contribute directly to protecting civic space and supporting human rights defenders while helping build sustainable organizational systems within an internationally connected advocacy organization. Professionals with strong coordination abilities, political awareness, and a passion for human rights work are encouraged to apply before the deadline on 21 May 2026 . title: Fully Funded PhD Positions in Switzerland 2026 at the Geneva Graduate Institute | Research on NATO Enlargement and Memory Politics
summary: The Geneva Graduate Institute has announced two fully funded PhD researcher positions within the groundbreaking research project, “Narrative Belonging: Memory Politics and NATO Enlargement in Europe.” Hosted by the Department of International History and Politics and the Centre on Conflict, Development and Peacebuilding (CCDP), this opportunity offers doctoral researchers the chance to conduct high-level academic research in Switzerland while contributing to a major international project on European security and historical narratives. The positions are scheduled to run from September 2026 to August 2030 and are funded according to SNSF doctoral researcher standards. Scholars interested in European history, memory studies, Cold War politics, and NATO enlargement are encouraged to apply before the deadline on 29 May 2026 (23:59 CET). About the Research Project The project explores how historical memory and political narratives have shaped NATO enlargement and Europe’s evolving security architecture since the end of the Cold War. Rather than focusing only on military or strategic perspectives, the research investigates how national memories, political identities, and symbolic practices influenced NATO accession processes. Key research areas include: The 1999 NATO accession of the Visegrád countries The 2004 enlargement involving the Vilnius group The recent accession of Finland and Sweden National narratives related to occupation, liberation, neutrality, and democratic transition NATO ceremonies, anniversaries, and symbolic political rituals The project places particular emphasis on Poland and the Baltic states, although other enlargement case studies may also be examined. Research Methods and Academic Environment Selected doctoral researchers will join an international and interdisciplinary academic environment at the Geneva Graduate Institute in Switzerland. The project uses several qualitative research methods, including: Archival research using NATO and national archives Media and discourse analysis Oral history interviews with diplomats and policymakers Digital humanities research through the creation of an open digital archive Researchers will contribute to collaborative project activities while simultaneously developing independent doctoral dissertations aligned with the project’s broader themes. The project is hosted by the Centre on Conflict, Development and Peacebuilding (CCDP), providing additional opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration and academic networking. Responsibilities of the Doctoral Researchers Successful applicants will: Participate fully in the doctoral programme and coursework requirements Conduct archival and historical research Assist with media analysis and interview-based research Contribute to the project’s digital archive development Engage in project workshops, conferences, and academic activities Develop their own doctoral dissertation connected to the project themes Benefits of the Fully Funded PhD Positions Selected candidates will receive: Full funding and salary aligned with SNSF doctoral standards Four-year doctoral contract from 2026–2030 Access to an internationally recognized academic institution Mentorship and supervision from experienced scholars Opportunities for international conferences and workshops Research travel and archival research support A multicultural and collaborative research environment in Geneva, Switzerland Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following criteria: Hold a Master’s degree in History or a related field Demonstrate strong interest in European history, memory studies, or security studies Possess excellent research and analytical skills Have strong English language proficiency Be capable of independent and collaborative research work Additional language skills, especially Polish or Baltic languages, are considered an advantage. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following documents in PDF format: Letter of motivation Research proposal (maximum 1000 words) Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic transcripts and diplomas Writing sample (MA thesis chapter or article manuscript) Names of two referees English language certificate (if applicable) Application Deadline The deadline to apply is 29 May 2026 (23:59 CET). Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed during the second week of June 2026. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their application through the official application portal of the Geneva Graduate Institute. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . For additional information, applicants may contact the Principal Investigator, Professor Jussi Hanhimaki, via email at jussi.hanhimaki@graduateinstitute.ch Discover more PhD opportunities, click here . The post Fully Funded PhD Positions in Switzerland 2026 at the Geneva Graduate Institute | Research on NATO Enlargement and Memory Politics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Disney Entertainment Television Writing Program 2027 Now Open for Applications
summary: Applications are officially open for the 2027 Disney Entertainment Television Writing Program , a prestigious initiative designed to support and develop the next generation of television writers. The highly competitive 12-month program provides emerging writers with professional development opportunities, industry exposure, mentorship, and potential staffing consideration on Disney Entertainment Television productions. Aspiring television writers looking to break into the entertainment industry now have the opportunity to learn directly from executives, producers, and creative professionals within one of the world’s leading entertainment companies. About the Disney Entertainment Television Writing Program The program is focused on helping talented writers strengthen their storytelling skills while gaining practical insight into the television industry. Participants receive structured career development and opportunities to engage with experienced industry professionals. The initiative is widely recognized as a pathway for emerging writers seeking long-term careers in television writing and production. What Participants Will Receive Selected participants will benefit from: A 12-month professional development experience Engagement with television executives and producers Industry mentorship and networking opportunities Career development workshops and training Consideration for staffing opportunities on Disney Entertainment Television series Why This Program Matters Breaking into television writing can be highly competitive, and programs like this provide invaluable access to industry networks, mentorship, and professional guidance. The Disney Entertainment Television Writing Program helps participants build practical industry knowledge while positioning them for future opportunities in scripted television. Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for: Emerging television writers Screenwriters and storytellers Creatives interested in scripted television production Writers seeking professional development in the entertainment industry Applicants should demonstrate strong storytelling ability, originality, and a passion for television writing. Application Deadline Applications close on: June 1, 2026 How to Apply For full program details, FAQs, and application instructions, visit here. For more Training opportunities, Visit OFY Here. The post Disney Entertainment Television Writing Program 2027 Now Open for Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Santander Scholarship | University Staff Development Grants 2026: £500 Professional Development Funding for UK University Staff
summary: Professional development is one of the most important investments any university staff member can make in their career. Whether you are an academic, researcher, administrator, technician, lecturer, support staff member, or part of professional services, staying updated with new skills and industry trends is essential for long-term growth and career advancement. To support this goal, the Santander Open Academy has launched the Santander Scholarship | University Staff Development Grants 2026 , a major funding opportunity designed specifically for staff working within UK higher education institutions. The programme will award 100 grants worth £500 each to eligible university staff members across the United Kingdom. These grants are intended to help recipients invest in training, conferences, professional accreditation, certified learning, and other development opportunities that can strengthen their careers and professional impact. Importantly, applicants do not need to be Santander customers to apply. Overview of the Santander University Staff Development Grants 2026 The Santander University Staff Development Grants 2026 is a national initiative created to promote professional growth among employees in the UK higher education sector. The programme recognizes that university staff across all departments contribute significantly to the success of institutions and deserve opportunities to improve their expertise, expand their networks, and build new competencies. The funding can be used flexibly, allowing recipients to choose development activities that best align with their professional goals. Key Details Programme Name: Santander Scholarship | University Staff Development Grants 2026 Host Organization: Santander UK Funding Amount: £500 Number of Grants Available: 100 Application Deadline: July 2, 2026 Eligible Country: United Kingdom Programme Type: Professional Development Grant Funding Usage Period: Within 12 months after award Application Mode: Online Selection Method: Random prize draw from eligible applications What the £500 Grant Can Be Used For One of the strongest aspects of this programme is its flexibility. Recipients can use the funding for a variety of professional development activities that contribute to career growth and skills enhancement. Eligible expenses include: 1. Training Courses and Certified Learning Applicants can use the grant to enroll in: Professional development courses Skills training workshops Online certification programmes Industry-recognized certifications Leadership training Digital skills programmes Technical training This allows university staff to stay competitive and relevant in rapidly evolving professional environments. 2. Conferences and Professional Events The grant can support participation in: Academic conferences Industry events Research symposiums Professional seminars Networking events Educational workshops Attending conferences helps professionals gain exposure to new ideas, emerging trends, and valuable networking opportunities. 3. Professional Memberships and Accreditation Fees Many professional fields require ongoing membership and accreditation to maintain professional standing. The funding can therefore cover: Membership fees for professional bodies Accreditation costs Licensing fees Professional registration expenses 4. Travel and Accommodation Where development activities involve travel, recipients can also use the grant for: Transportation expenses Accommodation costs Conference travel support Event-related travel arrangements However, travel expenses must be directly linked to a conference or professional event. Why This Opportunity Matters Professional development opportunities are often limited by financial constraints, especially for staff balancing work responsibilities and personal commitments. This programme provides a practical solution by reducing financial barriers and helping staff members invest in their future. Major Benefits of the Programme Career Advancement Participants can acquire new qualifications and competencies that improve promotion opportunities and career progression. Skill Development and Reskilling The programme supports both: Building new skills Updating outdated skills Reskilling for changing professional demands Access to Professional Networks Conferences and professional events create opportunities to connect with: Industry experts Researchers Educators Institutional leaders Professional peers Increased Professional Recognition Accreditation and professional memberships can strengthen credibility and recognition within a specific field. Flexible Use of Funds Unlike highly restrictive funding programmes, this grant allows recipients to choose development opportunities most relevant to their personal career goals. Who Can Apply? The programme is open to a broad range of university employees across the UK higher education sector. Eligible applicants include: Academic staff Researchers Professional services staff Technical staff Administrative staff Support staff Teaching staff Full-time employees Part-time employees Permanent staff Fixed-term employees Applicants must: Be aged 18 or older Be employed at a recognized UK higher education institution Provide a valid university staff ID number or equivalent identifier Apply for activities taking place within 12 months after receiving the grant Importantly, the development activity must not already be completed at the time of application. Application Timeline Applicants should pay close attention to the official programme timeline to avoid missing important dates. Important Dates Applications Opened: April 28, 2026 Application Deadline: July 2, 2026 Evaluation Period Ends: July 8, 2026 Selection Date: July 8, 2026 How Winners Are Selected Unlike many competitive grant programmes that rely heavily on essays or rankings, the Santander University Staff Development Grants use a random prize draw system . This means: All eligible applications are entered into the selection process Winners are randomly selected Universities are not involved in the selection process Santander independently manages the programme This approach helps create equal opportunities for eligible applicants regardless of role or seniority. How to Apply The application process is straightforward and designed to be accessible. Steps to Apply Visit the Santander Open Academy Official Website Register for an account if you do not already have one Complete the online application form Provide your university staff ID number Submit your application before July 2, 2026 CLICK HERE TO APPLY Additional Learning Opportunities Beyond the grant itself, applicants also gain access to Santander Open Academy’s learning ecosystem. Participants can explore: Free online courses On-demand learning programmes Professional development resources Training materials from global institutions This adds additional long-term value beyond the £500 funding support. Important Conditions to Remember Before applying, applicants should note several important conditions: Funding must be used within 12 months of receiving the award The grant cannot be used for activities already completed Only eligible UK university staff can apply Applicants must still be employed during the application and award period Terms and conditions apply Final Thoughts The Santander Scholarship | University Staff Development Grants 2026 represents an excellent opportunity for UK university staff members seeking to enhance their professional growth without significant financial burden. With flexible funding options, broad eligibility criteria, and a simple application process, the programme is designed to support career progression across the higher education sector. Whether you want to attend a conference, gain a new certification, join a professional body, or develop specialized skills, this £500 grant can help you move closer to your professional goals. Because only 100 grants are available, interested applicants should prepare and submit their applications before the July 2, 2026 deadline. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here . title: LSE Fellow in Economic and Social and Public Policy at the London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) 2026 in the UK
summary: The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) has announced an exciting career opportunity for early-career academics seeking to build a strong foundation in teaching and research within one of the world’s leading universities. Applications are now open for the prestigious LSE Fellow in Economic and Social and Public Policy position under the Department of Social Policy. This fully funded academic career development role offers successful candidates the opportunity to contribute to teaching, supervision, and internationally recognized research while working in one of the highest-ranked social policy departments globally. The position is based in London, United Kingdom, and is designed for ambitious scholars passionate about international social and public policy, social welfare systems, economics of public policy, inequality, migration, education, climate policy, and related areas. About the London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) The London School of Economics and Political Science is globally recognized for excellence in social sciences research, policy impact, and academic innovation. The Department of Social Policy at LSE is the longest-established department of its kind in the United Kingdom and consistently ranks among the best worldwide. According to the 2026 QS World University Rankings by Subject, the Department of Social Policy ranks 4th globally, highlighting its influence in shaping policy research and academic thought leadership. The department is known for its: Multidisciplinary approach International and comparative research focus Research activities across high-, middle-, and low-income countries Commitment to addressing pressing global social challenges Its major research strengths include: Poverty and inequality Work and welfare systems Education policy Care and social protection Women and gender studies Race, ethnicity, and migration Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Crime and criminal justice Climate and environmental policy Behavioural public policy Overview of the LSE Fellowship Position The LSE Fellowship is specifically designed as an entry pathway into an academic career. It provides early-career scholars with opportunities to strengthen their teaching portfolio, develop research output, and gain experience in a globally respected institution. The fellowship is a fixed-term appointment lasting two years, beginning in September 2026. Position Details Institution: London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) Department: Social Policy Location: London, United Kingdom Position Title: LSE Fellow in Economic and Social and Public Policy Contract Type: Fixed-term (2 years) Start Date: September 2026 Application Deadline: 31 May 2026 (23:59 UK Time) Salary and Benefits Successful candidates will receive a highly competitive salary package alongside excellent employment benefits. Salary Package Annual salary ranging from £43,277 to £51,714 Potential progression up to £55,497 Inclusive of London allowance Additional Benefits LSE also offers several employee benefits, including: Occupational pension scheme Generous annual leave Professional training and development opportunities Access to a world-class academic environment International networking and collaboration opportunities Exposure to leading policy researchers and institutions Key Responsibilities The selected LSE Fellow will contribute significantly to both teaching and research activities within the Department of Social Policy. Teaching Responsibilities Successful candidates will teach and support courses related to: Economics of social and public policy International social and public policy Undergraduate and postgraduate programmes BSc and MSc students in International Social and Public Policy Responsibilities may include: Delivering lectures and seminars Supervising student projects and dissertations Supporting curriculum development Engaging with virtual learning environments such as Moodle Participating in teaching-related administrative activities Research Responsibilities Candidates are also expected to actively contribute to LSE’s internationally recognized research culture through: Producing high-quality academic publications Conducting independent and collaborative research Participating in departmental research activities Contributing to policy debates and scholarly discussions Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this fellowship opportunity, applicants should meet several academic and professional requirements. Required Qualifications Applicants must: Have completed or be close to completing a PhD by the post start date Hold expertise in one of the following or related fields: Social Policy Public Policy Economics Political Science Sociology Related disciplines relevant to the role Teaching Requirements Applicants should demonstrate: Ability to teach economics of social and public policy Capacity to teach international social and public policy courses Experience teaching undergraduate and/or postgraduate students Research Expectations Candidates should show: Strong research potential Ability to produce internationally excellent publications Interest in international social and public policy research Additional Advantage The following experience will strengthen an application: Teaching-related administrative experience Experience using virtual learning platforms such as Moodle Important Eligibility Restriction LSE has clarified that applicants who have already been employed as an LSE Fellow for a total of three years are not eligible for this opportunity. This condition exists because the fellowship is intended strictly as a career development position for early-career academics. Why You Should Apply This fellowship presents a remarkable opportunity for emerging academics who wish to establish themselves in teaching and research within a globally respected institution. Major Advantages of the Fellowship Gain teaching experience at a top-ranked university Conduct research in an internationally recognized department Build academic networks and collaborations Improve publication portfolio Access excellent mentorship and development resources Enhance future academic career prospects For researchers interested in public policy, welfare systems, inequality, migration, or global social issues, this role provides an exceptional environment for intellectual growth and career advancement. Application Process Interested applicants must submit their applications through the official LSE online application portal. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review: The job description Person specification Application guidelines Supporting documentation requirements CLICK HERE TO APPLY Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: 31 May 2026 (23:59 UK Time) Late applications will not be accepted. Interview Information Shortlisted candidates will be invited for online interviews via Zoom. Interview Schedule Interviews are expected to take place during the week commencing 22 June 2026 For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . title: EU AMAL Project Grants 2026: Up to €40,000 Funding for Palestinian CSOs Advancing Gender & Disability-Inclusive Advocacy
summary: The European Union-funded AMAL – Amplifying Mobilization and Local Leadership Project has officially launched its 2026 Call for Proposals, offering critical funding and technical support for Palestinian civil society organizations working to strengthen gender equality, disability rights, inclusive governance, and equitable recovery efforts across Palestine. Implemented by Oxfam Novib in partnership with the Palestinian Medical Relief Society (PMRS) and the Atfaluna Society for Deaf Children (ASDC), the AMAL Project is a 36-month initiative running from January 2025 to December 2027. The program aims to empower local civil society organizations (CSOs), marginalized communities, women, youth, and persons with disabilities (PWDs) to actively shape inclusive governance and sustainable development processes in Palestine. This grant opportunity is particularly important for organizations seeking to influence humanitarian response, recovery, reconstruction, policy development, and community-led advocacy initiatives in the Gaza Strip, East Jerusalem, and the West Bank. About the EU AMAL Project The AMAL Project focuses on strengthening the leadership and operational capacity of Palestinian CSOs and marginalized communities by supporting inclusive services, advocacy initiatives, and recovery mechanisms. According to the guidelines, the project specifically targets women, youth, children, and persons with disabilities. The project’s broader objectives include: Enhancing empowerment and well-being among vulnerable populations through improved access to inclusive services. Supporting local CSOs and marginalized groups to influence key stakeholders and promote equitable recovery and reconstruction policies. Strengthening disability-inclusive and gender-responsive advocacy at community and policy levels. The initiative is designed to amplify local leadership and ensure that recovery and reconstruction processes meaningfully include marginalized voices, especially women and persons with disabilities. Funding Details The total funding available under this call amounts to €120,000 and will be distributed among 3–4 organizations. Individual grants will range between €30,000 and €40,000. Key Funding Information Total available funding: €120,000 Number of grants: 3–4 Grant size: €30,000–€40,000 Project duration: Up to 12 months Implementation period: August 2026 – July 2027 Geographic focus: Gaza Strip West Bank East Jerusalem Selected organizations will also participate in capacity-building activities, learning exchanges, and advocacy initiatives organized by Oxfam and project partners throughout the AMAL project cycle until December 2027. What Types of Organizations Can Apply? The call is open to Palestinian organizations with demonstrated experience in gender equality, disability rights, advocacy, and inclusive development. Eligible organizations include: Civil society organizations (CSOs) Women’s rights organizations (WROs) Community-based organizations (CBOs) Registered CSO networks Non-profit entities legally operating in Palestine Organizations must demonstrate: Experience in gender and disability advocacy Policy development expertise Stakeholder engagement capacity Strong partnerships and networks Financial and operational management systems Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must meet several mandatory requirements. Organizational Requirements Applicants must: Be legally registered and recognized by relevant ministries Operate as a not-for-profit entity Have active bank accounts Have no unresolved tax issues Maintain accounting and financial management systems Employ at least one accountant Demonstrate expertise in gender and disability inclusion advocacy Required documentation includes: Registration certificates Tax clearance certificates Audit reports for the past two years Bank account certificates Organizational policies (if available), including safeguarding and anti-fraud policies Priority Areas and Approaches Priority will be given to organizations and projects that adopt: Rights-Based and Inclusive Approaches Projects should ensure meaningful participation of women and persons with disabilities while incorporating intersectionality and inclusive governance practices. Evidence-Based Advocacy Organizations are encouraged to design interventions backed by data, research, and policy analysis to strengthen disability rights and gender inclusion in humanitarian recovery systems. Participatory Community Engagement Projects should actively involve: Youth-led organizations Community-based organizations Persons with disabilities Women-led groups throughout planning and implementation processes. Localization and Community Leadership The AMAL Project strongly prioritizes locally led solutions and community ownership. Eligible Activities Applicants are encouraged to design innovative and context-specific activities aligned with the project objectives. Examples include: Community-Based Inclusion Initiatives Programs demonstrating practical gender and disability inclusion in humanitarian recovery efforts. Income-Generating Projects Economic empowerment initiatives targeting women and youth with disabilities. Capacity-Building Programs Training for local organizations and women-led groups on disability-inclusive approaches and advocacy methods. Creative Advocacy Campaigns Activities such as: Digital storytelling Social media advocacy Cultural campaigns Public awareness initiatives promoting the inclusion of women and persons with disabilities in reconstruction processes. Policy Dialogues and Strategic Meetings Engagement sessions with government institutions, humanitarian actors, and local stakeholders to strengthen inclusive policymaking. Participation in Conferences and Advocacy Events Projects may include participation in regional or national advocacy initiatives related to gender rights, disability inclusion, and equitable recovery. Ineligible Activities and Costs Certain activities and costs are not eligible for funding. Ineligible Activities Individual scholarships Standalone training scholarships Ineligible Costs Examples include: Debt servicing Currency exchange losses Land or building purchases Unconditional cash transfers Salaries of national administration staff Credits to third parties Performance-based bonuses Evaluation Criteria Applications will be reviewed by a five-member Grant Selection Committee. Projects will be assessed based on: Project Selection Criteria Relevance Feasibility Sustainability Cost-effectiveness Community involvement and accountability Applicant Selection Criteria Organizational mandate Demonstrated expertise Experience in target areas Coordination and partnerships Project management capacity Financial management systems Ethical standards and safeguarding policies Applications are scored on a scale from 1 to 5, ranging from “Insufficient” to “Excellent.” Why This Opportunity Matters The AMAL Grant Scheme arrives at a critical moment for Palestinian civil society. By supporting locally led advocacy, inclusive governance, and disability rights initiatives, the program empowers organizations to shape recovery and reconstruction systems that genuinely reflect community needs. Beyond financial support, the program offers opportunities for: Long-term advocacy engagement Policy influence Capacity strengthening Regional networking Community leadership development Organizations working in gender equality, disability rights, humanitarian response, and inclusive governance should strongly consider applying. How to Apply Applications must be submitted electronically via email. Submission Email OPT.Subgrants@oxfam.org Subject Line Format Organization Name / EU AMAL Project Required Annexes Applicants must submit: Application Form – Narrative Application Form – Budget Eligibility Checklist Applications can be submitted in English or Arabic. All signed documents must be provided in both PDF and editable formats. Important Dates Questions deadline: 10 May 2026 Information sessions by Oxfam: 6 May 2026 Application deadline: 18 May 2026 Notification of selected applicants: Around 20 June 2026 Project implementation period: August 2026 – July 2027 Click HERE to learn more about the EU AMAL Project Grants 2026. For more global Grant opportunities, visit OFY HERE . The post EU AMAL Project Grants 2026: Up to €40,000 Funding for Palestinian CSOs Advancing Gender Disability-Inclusive Advocacy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026 at SOAS University of London: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity in Zoroastrian Studies
summary: The SOAS University of London has officially opened applications for the prestigious Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026, a fully funded opportunity designed for exceptional students interested in pursuing advanced studies connected to Zoroastrianism and related interdisciplinary fields. This scholarship offers full tuition coverage along with a maintenance stipend for one year, making it one of the most valuable master’s funding opportunities currently available for students passionate about Iranian, Middle Eastern, South Asian, religious, political, and cultural studies. Funded through the generous Shapoorji Pallonji endowment for scholarships in Zoroastrian Studies at SOAS, the award seeks to support academically outstanding students from around the world who are eager to explore Zoroastrianism through diverse academic lenses including history, anthropology, linguistics, religion, and cultural studies. The application deadline for the scholarship is 29 May 2026 . About the Scholarship The Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship is hosted by the SOAS Shapoorji Pallonji Institute of Zoroastrian Studies at SOAS University of London . The scholarship is available for students beginning full-time taught master’s programmes during the 2026/27 academic year. Selected scholars will receive: Full tuition fee coverage A maintenance stipend for living expenses Access to world-class academic resources at SOAS The opportunity to specialize in Zoroastrian Studies within a globally respected institution Exposure to interdisciplinary approaches to religion, politics, history, language, and society The scholarship aims to encourage innovative academic engagement with Zoroastrianism and related fields while supporting future researchers, scholars, and professionals in the humanities and social sciences. Eligible Master’s Programmes Applicants can choose from the following eligible one-year full-time master’s programmes: MA Iranian Studies MA Religion, Politics and Society MA South Asian Area Studies MA Middle Eastern Studies Students awarded the scholarship will also be required to complete: A compulsory 60-credit dissertation in Zoroastrianism The 30-credit module: “Zoroastrianism, Historical and Contemporary Perspectives” Additionally, students may choose to study an extra language module such as Avestan language studies. Scholarship Benefits The Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship offers substantial academic and financial support, including: 1. Full Tuition Coverage The scholarship fully covers tuition fees for the selected master’s programme. 2. Maintenance Stipend Recipients also receive financial support toward living costs during their studies in London. 3. Academic Development Students gain access to internationally respected faculty, specialized research opportunities, and interdisciplinary learning environments. 4. Research Opportunities The programme supports students interested in: Historical studies Religious studies Anthropology Linguistics Cultural studies Middle Eastern and South Asian studies 5. Global Recognition Studying at SOAS offers international academic exposure and networking opportunities within global research communities. Who Can Apply? The scholarship is open to both: Home students Overseas/international students Applicants from any country may apply provided they meet the academic and admission requirements. Academic Requirements To qualify, applicants should: Hold or expect to receive a first-class honours degree from a UK university or an international equivalent Demonstrate strong academic excellence through transcripts and references Have applied for an eligible master’s programme at SOAS Meet English language requirements if applicable Applicants with a 2:1 degree classification may still apply, although preference is generally given to candidates with first-class academic performance. Important Admission Conditions Applicants should note the following carefully: You do not need to receive an admission offer before submitting your scholarship application. However, you must obtain an unconditional admission offer before the scholarship selection panel meets. English language conditions must also be fulfilled before final consideration. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply for admission as early as possible because programme assessments may take: Up to 4 weeks under normal circumstances Up to 6 weeks during busy periods Late or incomplete scholarship applications will not be considered. Scholarship Deadline Scholarship Application Deadline: 29 May 2026 (12 noon UK local time) Applicants are advised to complete both: Their programme application Their scholarship application well before the deadline to avoid delays. How to Apply Step 1: Apply for an Eligible Master’s Programme Candidates must first submit an application for one of the eligible master’s programmes at SOAS. Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship After applying for admission, applicants can access the scholarship application through the SOAS admissions portal using their login credentials. Applicants who cannot see the scholarship option in the portal should verify that they meet all eligibility requirements. Candidate Assessment Process Applications will be reviewed based on: Academic excellence Personal statement quality Proposed area of study References and transcripts Overall suitability for Zoroastrian Studies research The scholarship selection panel will carefully assess each candidate’s academic potential and alignment with the objectives of the programme. Notification of Results The scholarship selection process is expected to conclude around the end of May 2026 or shortly afterward. Successful candidates will be contacted first. Unsuccessful applicants will be notified once all awards have been allocated. Why This Scholarship Matters The Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship represents a major opportunity for students interested in exploring religion, culture, history, politics, and society through an interdisciplinary and globally relevant perspective. With rising international tuition costs, fully funded opportunities like this are highly competitive and valuable. The programme also allows students to engage deeply with Zoroastrian traditions and their influence across regions such as Iran, South Asia, and the Middle East. For students seeking careers in academia, policy, cultural studies, international relations, religious studies, or heritage research, this scholarship can serve as a transformative academic pathway. How to Apply Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026 at SOAS University of London: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity in Zoroastrian Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: World Bank East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026: FutureMakers-Youth Driving Jobs Through Digital Innovation and Entrepreneurship
summary: The World Bank Group is set to host the highly anticipated East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026 , themed #FutureMakers: Youth Driving Jobs , bringing together young leaders, policymakers, business executives, development partners, and global experts to discuss the future of employment in the digital era. This major international forum is designed to empower young people across the East Asia and Pacific region by helping them understand emerging job trends, gain practical insights into digital transformation, explore entrepreneurship opportunities, and build meaningful professional networks. As economies continue evolving rapidly due to artificial intelligence, automation, and technological innovation, the forum serves as a strategic platform for preparing the next generation of youth to thrive in the changing labor market. About the East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026 The East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum is a large-scale regional event focused on addressing one of the most urgent issues facing young people today: the future of jobs. The forum seeks to equip youth with: Knowledge about labor market trends Skills needed for future careers Opportunities for collaboration and innovation Access to global conversations on employment and digital transformation The 2026 edition places strong emphasis on: Digital innovation Entrepreneurship AI-powered jobs Skills development Employment pathways Youth-led solutions Event Date 20 – 21 May 2026 (ET) Event Format The forum will be conducted in a hybrid format , allowing participants to join: Virtually from anywhere in the world In-person through designated event locations Registered participants will receive livestream details closer to the event date. The Growing Jobs Challenge in East Asia and Pacific One of the key highlights of the forum is its focus on the region’s widening employment gap. According to the event information: More than 320 million young people across East Asia and Pacific are expected to enter the workforce over the next decade Only about 110 million jobs are projected to be available This creates a gap of approximately 210 million job opportunities , posing significant economic and social challenges across the region. The forum aims to tackle these concerns by encouraging dialogue, innovation, collaboration, and practical solutions that can support sustainable youth employment. What Participants Can Expect The Youth Forum 2026 offers an engaging and highly interactive experience designed for emerging leaders and changemakers. 1. Understanding the Future Job Landscape Participants will gain valuable insights into: The future of work in East Asia and Pacific How digital transformation is reshaping industries Opportunities and risks in AI-driven economies Emerging career pathways for youth 2. Interactive Learning Sessions The forum will feature: Skills development workshops Entrepreneurship discussions Career readiness sessions Digital transformation training Collaborative problem-solving activities These sessions will be led by: Partner organizations Industry leaders Government representatives Development experts World Bank Group specialists 3. Networking Opportunities Attendees will have opportunities to: Connect with peers from across the region Meet professionals and business leaders Engage with policymakers and development experts Share ideas and innovative solutions Networking sessions are expected to help young participants build relationships that could support future collaborations, careers, and entrepreneurial ventures. 4. Exploring Digital and AI-Powered Careers A major focus of the event is the role of technology in creating employment opportunities. Participants will explore: AI-driven job opportunities Digital entrepreneurship Technology-enabled employment pathways Skills needed for the digital economy Apprenticeships and innovation ecosystems Why This Forum Matters The East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum is more than just a conference—it is a platform for youth empowerment and policy dialogue. The event is important because it: Addresses the growing youth unemployment crisis Encourages youth participation in economic transformation Promotes scalable employment solutions Supports entrepreneurship and innovation Bridges skills gaps through digital learning It also creates opportunities for youth voices to influence regional conversations on jobs, education, and economic development. Connection to the World Bank Global Youth Summit The event also serves as a lead-up initiative to the World Bank Group Global Youth Summit , scheduled for: 11 – 12 June 2026 This connection expands opportunities for participants to remain engaged in global youth development conversations beyond the regional forum. Who Should Attend? The forum is ideal for: Students Young professionals Entrepreneurs Youth leaders Innovators Career seekers Individuals interested in digital transformation and future jobs Participants interested in entrepreneurship, technology, workforce development, and social impact will particularly benefit from the event. Virtual Participation and Registration The event offers virtual participation options, making it accessible to participants from different countries and backgrounds. Interested individuals are encouraged to register early to secure participation access. Registration Link: Click here Benefits of Participating Participants can benefit from: Exposure to global employment trends Learning from industry and policy experts Enhanced understanding of future career opportunities Networking with regional and international stakeholders Insights into entrepreneurship and innovation Access to discussions on AI and digital jobs Final Thoughts The World Bank East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026 represents a timely and impactful opportunity for young people to prepare for the future workforce. As technology continues transforming economies globally, youth must adapt, innovate, and lead. Through interactive learning, networking, entrepreneurship discussions, and policy engagement, the forum aims to empower participants with the tools and knowledge needed to succeed in an increasingly digital world. Young changemakers across the region and beyond should strongly consider participating in this transformative event. For more information, click HERE Discover more global conference opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post World Bank East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026: FutureMakers-Youth Driving Jobs Through Digital Innovation and Entrepreneurship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) Junior Professional Program 2026 in Rabat, Morocco
summary: The Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) is inviting applications for its Junior Professional Program (JPP) 2026 in Rabat, Morocco. This highly competitive two-year professional program offers early-career graduates the opportunity to build expertise in international relations, economics, governance, climate policy, communications, and project management within one of Africa’s leading policy think tanks. For graduates seeking paid research jobs in Africa, public policy careers in Morocco, or structured entry-level roles in international development, the PCNS Junior Professional Program provides a strong pathway with the possibility of transitioning into a permanent contract. About the Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) The Policy Center for the New South is a Moroccan think tank dedicated to improving economic and social public policies that affect Morocco and the wider African continent as integral parts of the Global South. PCNS brings together researchers from diverse disciplines, publishes policy-relevant research, and collaborates with renowned institutional partners across multiple regions of the world. The institution plays an important role in strengthening evidence-based policymaking in Africa, particularly in areas such as governance, international relations, economics, climate, and energy. By fostering dialogue between policymakers, academia, and civil society, PCNS contributes to shaping forward-looking public policies that respond to regional and global challenges. Overview of the Junior Professional Program (JPP) The Junior Professional Program is a structured two-year job contract designed to enhance professional development and employability. Participants work in a professional environment that promotes interaction between researchers, government officials, development partners, and civil society actors. The program offers hands-on experience in key areas including: International relations and geopolitical analysis Economics and economic policy Governance and civil society research Climate and energy economics Agroeconomics Publications and editorial coordination Communications and partnerships Community management and finance Project management The JPP is specifically structured to strengthen both technical expertise and professional competencies through mentorship, collaboration, and exposure to high-level policy discussions. Permanent Contract Opportunity (CDI) A distinctive feature of the PCNS Junior Professional Program is the possibility of transitioning to a permanent contract (CDI) at any point during the two-year period, based on performance. This transition offers: Competitive remuneration Social benefits Long-term career prospects within the institution For candidates seeking stable policy research careers in Morocco or Africa, this pathway provides both immediate professional experience and long-term employment potential. Program Benefits Participants in the Junior Professional Program benefit from: Multidisciplinary expertise across economics, international relations, governance, and project management Direct involvement in projects that influence public policy in Morocco and across Africa Personalized mentorship and structured professional guidance Development of strong analytical, research, communication, and project management skills Opportunity to transition to a permanent contract based on performance The program is particularly suitable for graduates who aspire to work in think tanks, research institutions, international organizations, or public policy advisory roles. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Bachelor’s degree (four years) or Master’s degree (or equivalent) in international relations, economics, project management, or a related field Demonstrated interest in global issues and development in Africa Strong analytical, research, and/or project management skills Fluency in at least two languages, such as Arabic, English, French, or others Ability to work effectively in a team and communicate clearly Due to the high number of applications received, only shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews. Open Junior Professional Positions in Rabat, Morocco All positions are based in Rabat, Morocco. Most roles are offered under a two-year ANAPEC contract, with the possibility of transitioning to a permanent contract (CDI) depending on performance and the specific role. Junior Professional – Publications Editorial Coordinator Posted: February 5, 2026 Duration: ANAPEC 2 years, then CDI Language requirements: French and English Location: Rabat Junior Professional – Governance and Civil Society Research Posted: December 8, 2025 Duration: 2 years with ANAPEC, then permanent contract (CDI) Language requirements: Two fluent languages Location: Rabat Junior Professional – Climate Care Nexus Project Posted: November 24, 2025 Duration: 2 years with ANAPEC Language requirements: English, French, Arabic Location: Rabat Junior Professional – Agroeconomist Posted: August 28, 2025 Duration: 2 years with ANAPEC, then permanent contract (CDI) Language requirements: English, French, Arabic Location: Rabat Infographiste Junior Posted: December 29, 2025 Duration: ANAPEC 2 years, then CDI Language requirements: English, French, Arabic Location: Rabat Junior Professional – Energy Economics Posted: November 24, 2025 Duration: 2 years with ANAPEC, then permanent contract (CDI) Language requirements: English, French, Arabic Location: Rabat Why Apply to the PCNS Junior Professional Program The Policy Center for the New South Junior Professional Program is highly competitive due to its strong institutional reputation, structured professional development model, and potential for long-term employment. Key reasons to apply include: A paid two-year professional contract in a leading African think tank Exposure to high-level policy research and public policy development Strong mentorship and networking opportunities Multilingual and multicultural working environment Pathway to permanent employment in Morocco’s growing policy sector For graduates seeking research jobs in Africa, entry-level policy analyst roles, or international development careers in Morocco, the PCNS Junior Professional Program 2026 offers a strategic opportunity to build expertise while contributing to impactful public policy work. Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare a strong application highlighting academic qualifications, language proficiency, research interests, and commitment to African development and global policy issues. Apply Here for Open Junior Professional Positions in Rabat, Morocco The post Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) Junior Professional Program 2026 in Rabat, Morocco appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
